item.title: TotalEnergies Flags Strong Quarter as War Rattles Energy Market
item.description: TotalEnergies SE signaled a strong first quarter as surging energy prices and rising production outside the Middle East helped offset the impact of the Iran war.

item.title: Investors Look Past War to Focus on Fundamentals, Yardeni Says
item.description: Wall Street veteran Ed Yardeni said investors are looking past the war in the Middle East and focusing on market fundamentals.

item.title: Orban’s Defeat Ignites Euro Convergence Trade in Hungary
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s election defeat is extending a historic rally in Hungary’s bond market as his successor dangles the prospects of the country adopting the euro.

item.title: Tesco Says Middle East Conflict Causing Uncertainty for Outlook
item.description: Tesco Plc gave conservative guidance for this fiscal year as Britain’s largest grocer faces uncertainty over costs and the impact on households due to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Foreign Investors Keep Selling Chinese Bonds Despite Resilience
item.description: Foreign investors have pulled about $180 billion from Chinese bonds over the past year, underscoring how hard it is to retain overseas capital, even as the market held up better than most during the US-Iran war.

item.title: Private Credit's Biggest User Is in an Even Worse Place
item.description: As private credit managers mount a spirited defense of their industry to discourage investors from fleeing, they’ve found at least one persuasive argument for why much of the cash they lent to software firms at the start of the decade shouldn’t be at risk. If the leveraged buyouts they financed do get into difficulties because of competition from artificial intelligence, the private equity owners are first in line to lose money.

item.title: Rebound in South African Bonds Pays Off for Van Eck
item.description: Van Eck Associates Corp. started buying South African bonds during the biggest selloff on record, and is now reaping the reward with a return of triple the emerging-market average.

item.title: How Bond Market Volatility Hurts the UK Economy
item.description: The war in the Middle East has upended the UK bond market. Gilts have performed worse than almost all of their global peers since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, in large part because of the UK’s dependence on imported energy.

item.title: Sun Pharma, Grünenthal Are Said to Vie for US-Listed Organon
item.description: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. and Germany’s Grünenthal are among firms vying for New York-listed women’s health-care company Organon & Co., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: UK Economy Grew More Than Expected Before Start of Iran War
TSMC Raises 2026 Outlook in Sign of Confidence in AI Demand
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. raised its revenue outlook for 2026, an upbeat forecast that underscores the resilience of AI chip demand despite concerns about the economic fallout from the Middle Eastern conflict.

item.title: Indian LNG Importers Scoop Up Spot Shipments After Prices Recede
item.description: India’s liquefied natural gas importers have accelerated purchases from the spot market, taking advantage of a recent dip in prices, as the country looks to ease a supply crunch triggered by the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Iran-Linked Ships Take New Path To Trickle Into the Persian Gulf
item.description: At least two US-sanctioned, Iran-linked vessels made their way through the Strait of Hormuz and into the Persian Gulf on Thursday, taking what may be a new route from the United Arab Emirates even as a US blockade enters its third day.

item.title: Pimco Sees Opportunity in Europe’s Bonds After Selloff on War
item.description: Pacific Investment Management Co. is snapping up European government bonds after a sharp selloff sparked by the Middle East war.

item.title: Zimbabwe’s Upstart Dollar Bourse Overtakes 132-Year-Old Exchange
item.description: Zimbabwe’s dollar-denominated stock exchange has eclipsed the country’s 132-year-old main bourse, propelled by the biggest listing in the nation’s history.

item.title: Warsaw Bourse Seeks Closer Regional Ties to Stem IPO Exodus
item.description: The Warsaw Stock Exchange is calling for enhanced cooperation among its regional peers in a bid to stop the flight of major eastern European listings to western financial hubs.

item.title: Goldman’s Olson Sees Private Credit Market Growing Despite Fears
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s global head of alternatives for wealth expects private credit firms to keep drawing capital despite recent redemption episodes, due to the premium illiquid investments command.

item.title: BlackRock Strategist ‘Definitely’ Likes Korean Stocks on AI
item.description: BlackRock Inc. said South Korean equities are a key driver behind its latest upgrade of emerging-market stocks, citing sharp earnings growth and leverage to global semiconductor demand.

item.title: Dry-Bulk Shipping Rates Hit Four-Month Peak on Capesize Demand
item.description: A key gauge of bulk shipping rates climbed to the highest since early December, driven by a surge in demand and tightening vessel supply.

item.title: Goldman Favors North Asia’s Tech-Heavy Stock Markets Over South
item.description: North Asia’s tech-heavy equity markets offer better risk-reward than their South and Southeast Asian peers that are relatively more vulnerable to the oil shock triggered by the Iran war, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Bonds Overprice India’s Inflation Risk, Top Fund Manager Says
item.description: For one of India’s biggest bond fund managers, markets are overpricing the inflation risk from the Iran war and that is creating a buying opportunity.

item.title: Iran's So Far Away, Stocks Can Hit a Record
item.description: Despite great uncertainty from the war, the S&P 500 never fell 10% from its peak.

item.title: Así cambian los pueblos cuando llegan miles de millones en centros de datos
item.description: Las grandes tecnológicas dicen que el despliegue de la inteligencia artificial en Aragón está teniendo tanto éxito que debería servir de modelo para otras regiones de la Unión Europea. Para los vecinos, la realidad es más compleja.

item.title: What Happens When $90 Billion of Data Centers Come to Town
item.description: Big Tech companies say northern Spain’s AI buildout is going so well, it should be a model for other parts of the EU. For local residents, the reality is more complicated.

item.title: Alibaba Moves Onto Tencent’s Turf With New AI Model for 3D Video
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. launched a new AI model that can be used to develop games and generate videos simulating the real world, expanding its suite of products to challenge Tencent Holdings Ltd. as it looks to monetize the technology.

item.title: Dollar’s Real Competition Is Decades Away, Franklin’s Desai Says
item.description: The dollar’s dominance has been the subject of much scrutiny but Franklin Templeton sees the greenback remaining the currency of choice.

item.title: Australia Ramps Up Defense Spending, Sees Parallels to WWII Era
item.description: Australia will raise defense spending to 3% of gross domestic product by 2033, Defense Minister Richard Marles said, including increased outlays on drones as it responds to rising conflict globally and China’s military buildup.

item.title: Japan’s Nikkei 225 Set for Record Close, Erasing Iran War Losses
item.description: Japan’s Nikkei 225 Stock Average is headed for a record high close that would erase its losses from the Iran war on optimism the new peace talks may hasten the end to the conflict.

item.title: US House to Weigh Penalties on AI Model Copying by Chinese Firms
item.description: House Republicans are calling for US sanctions against Chinese entities that improperly extract results from leading US artificial intelligence models to develop their own competing systems, part of a widening effort by Congress to counter China in the global AI race.

item.title: India Small Caps Return to Pre-War Levels as Risk Mood Improves
item.description: A gauge of smaller shares has jumped over 12% in April.
